challenges.title: Operator Overloading
challenges.description: Write a C++ program that defines a class called `Vector2D` with double members `x` and `y`. Overload the `+` operator to add two `Vector2D` objects (adding their x and y components). Also overload the `<<` operator (as a friend function) to print a `Vector2D` in the format "(x, y)". In `main`, create two vectors, add them, and print all three using `cout`. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'class' in cpp and 'Vector2D' in cpp, 'Define a class called Vector2D'
assert 'operator+' in cpp.replace(' ', ''), 'Overload the + operator with operator+'
assert 'operator<<' in cpp.replace(' ', ''), 'Overload the << operator with operator<<'
assert 'friend' in cpp, 'The << operator overload should be a friend function'
assert 'ostream' in cpp, 'The << operator should take an ostream& parameter'
assert 'cout' in cpp, 'Print the vectors using cout and your overloaded << operator'
hints: Overload + as a member: Vector2D operator+(const Vector2D& other) { return Vector2D(x + other.x, y + other.y); }
Overload << as a friend: friend ostream& operator<<(ostream& os, const Vector2D& v) { os << "(" << v.x << ", " << v.y << ")"; return os; }

challenges.title: Static Members and the this Pointer
challenges.description: Write a C++ program that defines a class called `Counter` with a private int member `value` and a private static int member `instanceCount` that tracks how many `Counter` objects have been created. The constructor should increment `instanceCount` and accept an initial value. Include a method `add` that adds to `value` and returns `*this` (reference to the current object) for method chaining. Include a static method `getInstanceCount`. In `main`, create at least 2 `Counter` objects, demonstrate method chaining with `add`, and print the instance count. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'class' in cpp and 'Counter' in cpp, 'Define a class called Counter'
assert 'static' in cpp, 'Use a static member to track instance count'
assert 'instanceCount' in cpp or 'instance_count' in cpp, 'Name the static member instanceCount or instance_count'
assert '*this' in cpp or '* this' in cpp, 'Use *this to return a reference to the current object'
assert 'getInstanceCount' in cpp or 'get_instance_count' in cpp, 'Define a static method to get the instance count'
assert '.add(' in cpp, 'Demonstrate calling the add method'
assert cpp.count('.add(') >= 2 or '.add(' in cpp and ').add(' in cpp, 'Demonstrate method chaining by calling add at least twice'
hints: Declare static member: static int instanceCount; and initialize outside the class: int Counter::instanceCount = 0;
Return *this for chaining: Counter& add(int n) { value += n; return *this; }
